name: Twice Cooked Pork and Spicy Vegetables
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Pork Butt; in one piece
recipeInstructions:
1. Place pork butt in a saucepan and cover with water. Bring to a boil, then simmer for 30 minutes. Add more hot water if the level drops below the pork. Remove pork and cool it in its cooking water.
2. Soak mushrooms in warm water for 1 hour, squeeze out liquid, discard stems, and halve mushrooms.
3. Halve, seed, and core bell pepper; cut into pieces about 1 inch by 1 1/2 inches. Slice bamboo to match the size of bell pepper.
4. Peel carrot and slice on bias into ovals. Parboil carrots in water for 1 minute.
5. Slice remaining pork butt into rectangles the same size as bell pepper. Reserve 1/3 for another dish.
6. Strain mushroom liquid; combine with soy sauce, sugar, and salt.
7. Rinse bean curd in cold water and slice the same size as bell pepper.
8. Heat oil in a very hot wok. Stir-fry mushrooms, garlic, and ginger for 1 minute.
9. Add bell pepper, bamboo shoots, carrots, and pork; stir-fry for 1 minute.
10. Add bean curd and mushroom liquid; bring to boil.
11. On medium heat, cover wok and cook until bell pepper is bright green and crisp (1-2 minutes).
12. Restir cornstarch paste, then dribble into the liquid until it thickens while stirring.
13. Recombine with ingredients and serve.
